questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

options: A body at rest stays at rest; a body in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by external force
questions.solution: First law = law of inertia. Objects resist change in motion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the simply supported beam shown (central load W = 10 kN, span L = 8 m), the maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: For a central point load, M_max = WL/4 = 10×8/4 = 20 kN·m.

questions.solution: For cylinders: adding insulation first increases heat loss until r = k/h.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Helical spring deflection:
options: δ = 8FD³N / (Gd⁴). N = number of active coils
questions.solution: Stiffness k = F/δ = Gd⁴/(8D³N). Series: 1/k_eq = Σ1/k_i. Parallel: k_eq = Σk_i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Knocking in SI engines is caused by:
questions.solution: Knocking (SI): auto-ignition of unburnt end gas ahead of flame.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensible heating/cooling:
options: Temperature change only. No moisture change. Horizontal on psych chart
questions.solution: Sensible: follows constant ω line. Latent: constant T (humidification/dehumidification).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Welding is:
options: Joining materials by fusion (with or without filler)
questions.solution: Welding: arc (SMAW, GMAW, GTAW), gas (oxyacetylene), resistance (spot, seam), solid-state (friction, ultrasonic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Case hardening:
options: Hardens the surface while keeping core tough (carburizing, nitriding, cyaniding)
questions.solution: Carburizing: add C to surface (850-950°C). Nitriding: add N (500-550°C, no quench).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Whirling of shafts occurs when:
options: Rotational speed matches the natural frequency of the shaft
questions.solution: Whirling speed / critical speed = ω_n = √(k/m). Operating above critical speed is possible.

questions.stem: Bernoulli's equation is fundamentally a statement of:
options: Conservation of energy along a streamline
questions.solution: It balances pressure, kinetic and potential energy per unit weight along a streamline (no losses).

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: EGR reduces:
options: NOx formation by lowering peak temperature
questions.solution: EGR: dilutes charge with exhaust. Lower peak T reduces NOx.

questions.solution: On principal planes, shear stress τ = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Balancing of rotating masses is done primarily to:
questions.solution: Unbalanced rotating masses create centrifugal forces causing vibration; balancing cancels them.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a Rankine cycle, steam is condensed:
questions.solution: Rankine: 1-2 isentropic pump, 2-3 constant pressure boiler, 3-4 isentropic turbine, 4-1 constant pressure condenser.

questions.stem: Pump specific speed:
options: N_s = N√Q / H^0.75. Identifies pump type (centrifugal, mixed, axial)
questions.solution: N_s: radial (500-4000), mixed (4000-10000), axial (>10000). Affinity laws.

options: Flux-coated electrode. Slag protects weld pool. Versatile, portable
questions.solution: SMAW: DC or AC. Electrode classification E6010, E7018. Slag removal needed between passes.

questions.stem: The pressure angle in a cam:
options: Angle between the normal to the pitch curve and the follower velocity
questions.solution: Pressure angle affects side thrust. Lower pressure angle = better force transmission.
